extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains both a long plateau above 0.75 and a long plateau below 0.25.  
R2: The high plateau and the low plateau are separated in time (i.e., do not overlap), so the signal clearly switches between high and low once.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long plateau above 0.75.  
R2: Outside this high plateau, the rest of the series stays around 0.45–0.55, without any long segment below 0.25.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long plateau below 0.25.  
R2: Outside this low plateau, the rest of the series stays around 0.45–0.55, without any long segment above 0.75.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau at a high level (around 0.8) and one long plateau at a low level (around 0.2), separated by sharp transitions.
R2: In addition to the long plateau, there is a short peak (brief excursion to high level) occurring near one edge of the series (either at the beginning or at the end).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long plateau at a high level (around 0.8–0.9) in the middle of the time axis, with sharp rises and drops.
R2: Outside this high plateau, both the beginning and the end segments stay near a mid‑level baseline (around 0.48–0.5) without additional peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long plateau at a low level (around 0.15–0.2) in the middle of the time axis, with sharp drops into and rises out of this low region.
R2: Outside this low plateau, the beginning and end segments stay near a higher baseline (around 0.48–0.5), and there is at least one short, sharp dip (brief excursion to very low value) within one of these high‑baseline regions.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at a high level (around 0.8) in the middle.
R2: In addition to that plateau, there is a second, shorter excursion in the opposite direction (either an early high bump before a long low dip, or a late high bump after a long low dip).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single, long high plateau (around 0.8–0.9) in the center.
R2: Outside that high plateau, the series stays roughly flat around the baseline level (around 0.45–0.5) without any other large peaks or deep dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long, deep low plateau (around 0.1–0.2) in the center.
R2: In addition to that central dip, there is at least one extra sharp, narrow deep dip (a spike downward) either before or after the long low plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at a high level (around 0.8) and elsewhere stays around a mid level (around 0.5).  
R2: In addition to the long high plateau, there is a short plateau whose level is opposite to the long one (high when the long one is low, or low when the long one is high).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau (around 0.8–0.9) centered in time, with values clearly above the baseline.  
R2: Outside this plateau, the signal remains near a constant baseline level (around 0.48–0.5) without additional large peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (around 0.1–0.2) in the middle, much lower than the baseline.  
R2: Outside this long low plateau, there is at least one sharp, narrow dip from the higher baseline down toward the same low level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at a high value (around 0.8) located in the middle of the time axis.
R2: In addition to the long plateau, there is a second, much shorter excursion (either a peak above or a dip below the baseline) located near one of the ends (early or late in time).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series contains a single long plateau at a high value (around 0.8–0.9) located in the middle of the time axis.
R2: Outside this plateau, the signal stays close to a constant baseline (around 0.48–0.50) with no other strong peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at a low value (around 0.1–0.2) located in the middle of the time axis.
R2: On both sides (early and late in time) there are short, sharp dips from a higher baseline (around 0.45–0.50) down to much lower values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains both one long plateau above 0.75 and one long valley below 0.25.  
R2: The long plateau and the long valley are separated in time (one occurs in the middle of the series, the other near one of the ends).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long plateau above 0.75.  
R2: Outside this plateau, the values stay roughly around 0.45–0.55 without any long valley below 0.25.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long valley below 0.25.  
R2: Outside this valley, the values stay roughly around 0.45–0.55 and there is no long high plateau above 0.75.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long central plateau that is clearly separated from the baseline (either higher than baseline by a large step or lower than baseline by a large step).
R2: In addition to the long central plateau, there is a second, shorter plateau of opposite polarity (short “bump” above baseline when the central part is below, or short “dip” below baseline when the central part is above).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long plateau that is higher than the baseline (one broad “high” block).
R2: Outside this high plateau, the signal remains close to a constant baseline without any additional pronounced bumps or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long central plateau that is lower than the baseline (one broad “low” block).
R2: In addition to this long low plateau, there is at least one extra short, sharp dip below the surrounding baseline (a narrow “spike down”).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains both (a) a long plateau clearly above the baseline (around the middle of the series) and (b) at least one short, sharp spike above the baseline near one end.
R2: The baseline level is roughly constant and intermediate (around 0.45–0.55), and outside the spike/plateau the signal quickly returns to this baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single, long, flat plateau above the baseline, starting and ending roughly in the middle of the time axis, with no additional spikes before or after it.
R2: Before and after the plateau, the signal stays near a constant baseline (around 0.45–0.55) with only small noise and no pronounced dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long, flat low segment (a “valley”) clearly below the baseline, occupying the central part of the time axis.
R2: In addition to the long valley, there is at least one short, sharp dip below the baseline near one end, and outside dips/valley the series stays on a higher baseline (around 0.45–0.5).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains both a long high plateau (values clearly above the baseline) and a long low valley (values clearly below the baseline), each occupying a substantial central portion of the time axis.
R2: In addition to the long plateau and valley, there is at least one extra short peak or short valley near one end of the series (an “extra bump” on either the left or right side).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one single long high plateau in the middle of the time axis, with values clearly above the baseline and with flat-ish start and end.
R2: Outside this central plateau (both before it starts and after it ends), the values stay close to a common baseline level, without any additional pronounced peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low valley in the middle of the time axis, with values clearly below the baseline and with flat-ish start and end.
R2: In addition to this central valley, there is at least one extra short, sharp dip on one side (or both sides) of the valley, where the value briefly drops well below the surrounding baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau clearly above 0.7 located roughly in the middle of the time axis.  
R2: In addition to the long middle plateau, there is a shorter excursion (either above 0.7 or below 0.3) near one of the ends of the series.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long plateau clearly above 0.7 centered in the middle of the time axis.  
R2: Outside this central high plateau, values stay in a narrow band around 0.48 (no other pronounced peaks or dips).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low plateau clearly below 0.25 located roughly in the middle of the time axis.  
R2: Outside this central low plateau, there is at least one narrow, sharp dip below 0.25 near one of the ends.
